provenance.quote:
## Important Notes

- **Admin Required**: This tool requires admin permissions
- **Data Format**: Pass `data` as native objects, NOT stringified JSON
- **Flow Execution**: Flows with `operations` array will include their operations
- **Webhook URL**: After creating webhook trigger, URL is `/flows/trigger/<flow-id>`
- **Event Blocking**: Filter events pause transaction until flow completes
- **Logs**: Flow executions are logged (check `accountability` setting)
</important_notes>

<common_mistakes>

## Restrictions

- Cannot operate on `directus_*` collections
- Respects user permissions/RBAC
- Delete operations may be environment-disabled

### Collection Structure

```json
{
	"collection": "products",
	"meta": {
		"collection": "ai_prompts",
		"icon": "inventory_2", // Any Material Symbols icons
		"note": "Main product catalog with inventory tracking" // Helpful 1 sentence description
		"color": "#6366F1", // Color shown in content module sidebar
		"singleton": false, // Single-item collections (settings, globals)
		"hidden": false, // Hide from navigation
		"accountability": "all", // Track who activity and revisions (`"all"`, `"activity"`, `null`)
		"sort_field": "sort", // Default sorting field (auto-creates if needed)
		"archive_app_filter": true, // Enable soft delete in the app
		"archive_field": "status", // Field used for archiving (status, deleted_at, etc.)
		"archive_value": "archived", // Value that marks items as archived
		"unarchive_value": "published", // Value that marks items as active
		"display_template": "{{name}} - ${{price}}",
		"versioning": false, // Enable content versioning for this collection
		"sort": 2, // Sort order for this collection
		"group": null, // Parent collection (use to group and nest collections in data model)
		"collapse": "open" // Default collection to expanded or collapsed if child collections
		"preview_url": "https://store.example.com/products/{{slug}}", // Live preview URL to view items within collection - supports using template variables
		"translations": [
			{
				"language": "en-US",
				"translation": "Products",
				"singular": "product",
				"plural": "products"
			},
			{
				"language": "es-ES",
				"translation": "Productos",
				"singular": "producto",
				"plural": "productos"
			}
		],
	}
}
… (+13 more lines)

### Translations Field Example

```json
{
	"collection": "posts",
	"field": "translations",
	"type": "alias",
	"schema": null,
	"meta": {
		"collection": "posts",
		"field": "translations",
		"special": ["translations"],
		"interface": "translations",
		"options": {
			"userLanguage": true,
			"defaultOpenSplitView": true
		},
		"display": "translations",
		"display_options": {
			"template": "{{title}}", // Field to display from the translated collection (ie post title)
			"languageField": "name" // Name of the language field from the languages collection
		},
		"sort": 22,
		"width": "full"
	}
}
```

**Note**: Alias fields don't need a schema object since they're virtual. </relationship_fields>

	"field": "id",

	"meta": { "hidden": true, "readonly": true, "interface": "input", "special": ["uuid"] },

fields.conditions[].name: string — description of the condition
fields.conditions[].rule: object — filter rules using Directus filter syntax (_null, _eq, _neq, _in, _and, _or)

provenance.quote:
### File Field Example

```json
{
	"collection": "posts",
	"field": "featured_image",
	"type": "uuid",
	"schema": {
		"name": "featured_image",
		"table": "posts",
		"data_type": "uuid",
		"is_nullable": true,
		"foreign_key_schema": "public",
		"foreign_key_table": "directus_files",
		"foreign_key_column": "id"
	},
	"meta": {
		"collection": "posts",
		"field": "featured_image",
		"special": ["file"],
		"interface": "file-image",
		"options": {
			"folder": "post-images"
		},
		"display": "image",
		"display_options": null,
		"sort": 1,
		"width": "half",
		"required": false,
		"group": "media"
	}
}
```

### Files Field Example

```json
{
	"collection": "posts",
	"field": "gallery",
	"type": "alias",
	"schema": null,
	"meta": {
		"collection": "posts",
		"field": "gallery",
		"special": ["files"],
		"interface": "files",
		"options": null,
		"display": "related-values",
		"display_options": null,
		"sort": 4,
		"width": "full"
	}
}
```

fields.group: string — group related fields into collapsible sections (must be used with alias group fields)

provenance.quote:
### Layout

- **width**: `"half"` (380px max), `"full"` (760px max, default), `"fill"` (no limit)
- **sort**: Field order in forms
- **group**: Group related fields into collapsible sections (must be used with `alias` group fields)

### M2A Field Example

```json
{
	"collection": "pages",
	"field": "blocks",
	"type": "alias",
	"schema": null,
	"meta": {
		"collection": "pages",
		"field": "blocks",
		"special": ["m2a"],
		"interface": "list-m2a",
		"options": {},
		"display": "related-values",
		"display_options": {
			"template": "{{collection}}"
		},
		"sort": 8,
		"width": "full"
	}
}
```

fields.hidden: boolean — hidden from UI (still in API)

provenance.quote:
### Meta Properties

- **required**: Must have value
- **readonly**: Cannot edit after creation
- **hidden**: Hidden from UI (still in API)
- **validation**: Custom validation rules
- **validation_message**: Custom error messages
- **note**: Context for non-obvious fields

### Schema Properties

- **default_value**: Default for new items
- **is_nullable**: Can be null
- **is_unique**: Must be unique
- **length**: Max length for strings

fields.special: array of special behavior flags: uuid (auto-generate UUID), user-created (track creating user), date-created (track creation time), user-updated (track last editor), date-updated (track last edit time), cast-json (cast JSON strings to objects)

provenance.quote:
### Special Fields

- `special: ["uuid"]`: Auto-generate UUID
- `special: ["user-created"]`: Track creating user
- `special: ["date-created"]`: Track creation time
- `special: ["user-updated"]`: Track last editor
- `special: ["date-updated"]`: Track last edit time
- `special: ["cast-json"]`: Cast JSON strings to objects </field_configuration>

<translations>
Field names can (and should be) translated for editors using the app.
Check for `languages` collection first, then add field translations based on which languages are stored in DB. IF not 99% sure, confirm with user first.

```json
"translations": [
  {"language": "en-US", "translation": "Title"},
  {"language": "es-ES", "translation": "Título"}
]
```

</translations>

<display_templates> Display templates can be customized used to enhance the UX for editors.

```json
"display": "related-values",
"display_options": {
  "template": "{{first_name}} {{last_name}}"
}
```

**Display types**: `raw`, `formatted-value`, `labels`, `datetime`, `user`, `file`, `related-values` </display_templates>

## Actions

- **`read`**: List/query metadata or get specific items by ID
- **`update`**: Modify existing metadata
- **`delete`**: Remove files by keys
- **`import`**: Import a file from a URL and create or update its file data

fields.focal_point_x: integer, pixels from left edge of original image
fields.focal_point_y: integer, pixels from top-left corner of original image

validation.focal_point_x: ensures cropped images keep subject visible
validation.focal_point_y: ensures cropped images keep subject visible

fields.data[].url: string — URL to import file from
fields.data[].file.title: string

fields.title: display title
fields.focal_point_x: horizontal focal point in pixels from left edge
fields.focal_point_y: vertical focal point in pixels from top edge

provenance.quote:
## File Metadata Fields

- `id`: Unique identifier
- `storage`: Storage adapter used
- `filename_disk`: Actual filename on disk
- `filename_download`: Suggested download filename
- `title`: Display title
- `type`: MIME type (e.g., "image/jpeg", "application/pdf")
- `folder`: Parent folder ID
- `uploaded_by`: User who uploaded
- `uploaded_on`: Upload timestamp
- `modified_by`: Last modifier
- `modified_on`: Last modification time
- `filesize`: Size in bytes
- `width`/`height`: Dimensions for images (in pixels)
- `duration`: Length for video/audio
- `description`: File description
- `location`: Geo-location data
- `tags`: Array of tag strings (e.g., ["product", "red", "handbag"])
- `metadata`: Additional metadata object
- `focal_point_x`: Horizontal focal point (in pixels from left edge)
- `focal_point_y`: Vertical focal point (in pixels from top edge)

fields.query.filter._and: array of filter conditions combined with AND
fields.query.filter.filesize._lt: less than for file size in bytes
fields.query.filter.uploaded_on._gte: greater than or equal for upload date, supports $NOW(-7 days) syntax

fields.query.fields: array of field names to return (e.g. id, title, type, filesize, width, height)

provenance.quote:
### Get Single File Metadata

```json
{
	"action": "read",
	"keys": ["file-uuid-here"]
}
```

fields.keys: array of file UUIDs (single file update)

validation.data: must be passed as native objects, not stringified JSON

provenance.quote:
## Key Points

- **ALWAYS pass data as native objects**, NOT stringified JSON
- **Metadata only**: This tool manages file metadata, not file content or uploads
- **Permissions**: Respects Directus access control
- **Arrays required**: `keys` and `tags` must be arrays: `["item"]` not `"item"`
- **Performance**: Large files handled automatically but may impact performance

fields.name: string (required)
fields.operation: UUID of first operation (optional, set after creating operations)

provenance.quote:
## Flow Data Structure

All flows share these core fields for creation:

- `name` (required) - Flow display name
- `trigger` (required) - Trigger type: `event`, `webhook`, `schedule`, `operation`, `manual`
- `status` - `active` or `inactive` (default: `active`)
- `accountability` - `all`, `activity`, or `null` (default: `all`)
- `icon` - Icon identifier (optional)
- `color` - Hex color code (optional)
- `description` - Flow description (optional)
- `options` - Trigger-specific configuration object (optional)
- `operation` - UUID of first operation (optional, set after creating operations) </core_fields>

<crud_actions>

validation.options: must be inside the data object (inside the flow object), not at the top-level action object
validation.data: must be passed as native objects not stringified JSON
validation.name: required for creation

fields.$last: previous operation result (avoid — breaks when operations are reordered)

provenance.quote:
## Data Chain Access

Operations can access data using `{{ variable }}` syntax:

- `{{ $trigger.payload }}` - Trigger data
- `{{ $accountability.user }}` - User context
- `{{ $env.VARIABLE_NAME }}` - Environment variables
- `{{ operation_key }}` - Result from specific operation (recommended)
- `{{ operation_key.field }}` - Specific field from operation result
- `{{ $last }}` - Previous operation result (⚠️ avoid - breaks when reordered)

**Always use operation keys** for reliable flows. If you reorder operations, `$last` will reference a different
operation. </data_chain_warning>

<real_world_examples>

### Event Hook Trigger

Responds to data changes and system events

```json
{
	"trigger": "event",
	"options": {
		"type": "filter", // filter (blocking) | action (non-blocking)
		"scope": ["items.create", "items.update"],
		"collections": ["orders", "products"],
		"return": "process_data" // For filter only: <operationKey>|$all|$last (avoid $last)
	}
}
```

**Common Scopes**:

- `items.create`, `items.update`, `items.delete` - Data operations
- `auth.login` - User authentication
- **Note**: Multiple scopes trigger flow for ANY matching event

fields.options.fields: array of field definitions with field, type, name, meta properties

fields.options.return: data to return to calling flow: <operationKey>|$all|$last (avoid $last)

fields.options.cron: CRON expression in format: second minute hour day month weekday

fields.webhookUrl: after creating webhook trigger, URL is /flows/trigger/<flow-id>
fields.options.async: boolean — true = immediate response, false = wait for completion

<common_mistakes>
---
[line 127] ### Webhook Trigger

```json
{
	"trigger": "webhook",
	"options": {
		"method": "POST", // GET|POST
		"async": false, // true = immediate response, false = wait for completion
		"return": "transform_result", // Response body: <operationKey>|$all|$last (avoid $last)
		"cache": false // Cache GET responses
	}
}
```

validation.keys: must be array even for single items
validation.tags: must be array e.g. ["tag1", "tag2"] not "tag1, tag2"

provenance.quote:
## Mistakes to Avoid

1. **Remember** that `keys` expects an array even for single items
2. **Tags must be arrays**: Use `["tag1", "tag2"]` not `"tag1, tag2"`

fields.storage: folders are virtual — not mirrored with the storage adaptor, only stored in the database

provenance.quote:
## Important Notes

- **Folders are virtual**: Folders are not mirrored with the storage adaptor, only stored in the database.
- **Permissions**: Respects Directus access control - only accessible files are returned. If you don't see something
  that the user says you should have access to, it could be a permissions issue.
- **Folder Hierarchy**: Deleting a folder requires it to be empty or will cascade based on settings

fields.M2A_field: array of objects each with collection (string: target collection name) and item (object: item data or existing id)

fields.nested_create: pass object to create nested related item
fields.link_existing: pass UUID string to link existing item
pass array of primary keys to link existing items
fields.create_update_delete: pass object with create (array of new items), update (array with id), delete (array of ids) sub-keys

fields.transactional: batch operations are transactional — all succeed or all fail
fields.primary_keys: primary keys returned from create/update for chaining
fields.validation: validation errors thrown before database operations

provenance.quote:
### Performance & Safety

- **Use `fields`** to minimize payload size
- **Apply `limit`** for large result sets (default: no limit)
- **Batch operations** are transactional - all succeed or all fail
- **Primary keys returned** from create/update operations for chaining
- **Validation errors** are thrown before database operations

validation.required_fields: must include all required fields per collection schema
validation.field_types: validate field types match schema definitions
validation.singleton: check collection.singleton: true for singleton collections
validation.relations: verify relation targets exist before linking; or create new items by nesting
validation.schema: must fully understand collection schema before creating items

fields.data: array of item objects; supports nested relation creation (object creates nested), mix of existing IDs and new objects for M2M

provenance.quote:
[line 263] ### Before Creating Items

1. **Check required fields** in schema
2. **Validate field types** match schema definitions
3. **Check for singleton collections** (`collection.singleton: true`)
4. **Verify relation targets exist** before linking. You can also create new relation items by nesting

---
[line 52] ### Creating Items

- ALWAYS make sure you fully understand the collection's schema before trying to create items.

**✅ GOOD - Single with Relations:**

```json
{
	"action": "create",
	"collection": "posts",
	"data": [
		{
			"title": "New Post",
			"author": { "name": "John Doe", "email": "john@example.com" }, // Creates nested
			"categories": [1, 2, { "name": "New Category" }], // Mix existing + new
			"status": "draft"
		}
	]
}
```

**✅ GOOD - Batch Create:**

```json
{
	"action": "create",
	"collection": "posts",
	"data": [
		{ "title": "Post 1", "author_id": 1 },
		{ "title": "Post 2", "author_id": 2 }
	]
}
```

**❌ BAD - Missing Required Fields:**

```json
// Don't create without checking schema first
{ "title": "Post" } // Missing required fields like status
```
… (+1 more lines)

additionalSources.quote:
### Creating Items

- ALWAYS make sure you fully understand the collection's schema before trying to create items.

**✅ GOOD - Single with Relations:**

```json
{
	"action": "create",
	"collection": "posts",
	"data": [
		{
			"title": "New Post",
			"author": { "name": "John Doe", "email": "john@example.com" }, // Creates nested
			"categories": [1, 2, { "name": "New Category" }], // Mix existing + new
			"status": "draft"
		}
	]
}
```

**✅ GOOD - Batch Create:**

```json
{
	"action": "create",
	"collection": "posts",
	"data": [
		{ "title": "Post 1", "author_id": 1 },
		{ "title": "Post 2", "author_id": 2 }
	]
}
```

**❌ BAD - Missing Required Fields:**

```json
// Don't create without checking schema first
{ "title": "Post" } // Missing required fields like status
```
… (+1 more lines)

fields.deep: object keyed by relation field name; supports _filter, _sort, _limit per relation

validation.keys: always require explicit keys — never delete by query alone
validation.confirmation: must always get written confirmation from user before deleting
validation.cascade: validate cascade behavior for relationships before deleting
validation.soft_delete: consider soft delete if collection has status field

provenance.quote:
[line 292] ### Error Patterns to Avoid

- Creating without checking required fields through `scehma(keys: ["collection"])`
- Updating without keys or with invalid primary keys
- Deleting system collections (directus\_\*)
- Assuming field names without schema verification
- Missing foreign key references in relations
- Exceeding mutation limits in batch operations

---
[line 146] ### Deleting Items

- ALWAYS get written confirmation with the user before deleting any items.

**✅ GOOD - Delete by Keys:**

```json
{
	"action": "delete",
	"collection": "posts",
	"keys": ["uuid-1", "uuid-2"]
}
```

**❌ BAD - Delete All (Dangerous):**

```json
// Never delete without keys - use query to get keys first
{
	"action": "delete",
	"collection": "posts" // Will fail - keys required
}
```

---
[line 277] ### Before Deleting Items

1. **Always require explicit keys** - never delete by query alone
2. **Check for related data** that might be affected
3. **Validate cascade behavior** for relationships
4. **Consider soft delete** if collection has status field

additionalSources.quote:
### Deleting Items

- ALWAYS get written confirmation with the user before deleting any items.

**✅ GOOD - Delete by Keys:**

```json
{
	"action": "delete",
	"collection": "posts",
	"keys": ["uuid-1", "uuid-2"]
}
```

**❌ BAD - Delete All (Dangerous):**

```json
// Never delete without keys - use query to get keys first
{
	"action": "delete",
	"collection": "posts" // Will fail - keys required
}
```

### Before Deleting Items

1. **Always require explicit keys** - never delete by query alone
2. **Check for related data** that might be affected
3. **Validate cascade behavior** for relationships
4. **Consider soft delete** if collection has status field

fields.fields: array of field selectors; supports dot notation for relations (e.g. author.name), wildcard (categories.*), and M2A relations (sections.item:headings.title)

provenance.quote:
### Field Selection (Always Use)

```json
{ "fields": ["title", "status", "author.name", "categories.*"] }
```

For M2A relations: `"sections.item:headings.title", "sections.item:paragraphs.body"`

fields.date_functions: year(field), month(field), day(field), hour(field) — usable in filter
fields.groupBy: array of field names to group by

validation.schema: always call schema() first to discover collections; examine specific schemas with schema(keys: ["collection"]) for field details; never guess field names — always use exact names from schema

provenance.quote:
### Schema-First Pattern (Critical)

1. **Always call `schema()` first** to discover collections
2. **Examine specific schemas**: `schema(keys: ["collection"])` for field details based on users query
3. **Follow relation chains**: Check `relation.related_collections` in field definitions when it's relevant to your task
4. **IMPORTANT: Never guess field names** - Always use exact names from schema. If you're not 100% sure, ask the user

fields.read: singleton read uses action=read with collection name and query; no keys needed
fields.update: singleton update uses action=update with collection name and data; no keys needed
fields.regular_collections: require keys for update/delete; return arrays
fields.singleton_collections: no keys needed; return single objects; auto-upsert behavior

provenance.quote:
[line 170] ### Singleton Collections

**✅ GOOD - Singleton Read:**

```json
{
	"action": "read",
	"collection": "settings", // Singleton collection
	"query": { "fields": ["site_name", "logo"] }
}
```

**✅ GOOD - Singleton Update:**

```json
{
	"action": "update",
	"collection": "settings",
	"data": { "site_name": "New Name" } // No keys needed for singleton
}
```

---
[line 301] ### Singleton vs Regular Collections

**Regular Collections**: Require keys for update/delete, return arrays **Singleton Collections**: No keys needed, return
single objects, auto-upsert behavior

additionalSources.quote:
### Singleton vs Regular Collections

**Regular Collections**: Require keys for update/delete, return arrays **Singleton Collections**: No keys needed, return
single objects, auto-upsert behavior

fields.translations: array of translation objects with languages_code and translated fields
fields.translations.languages_code: BCP 47 language code e.g. en-US, fr-FR

validation.bulk_same_data: use keys parameter for bulk updates with same data
validation.batch_different_data: use data array for batch updates with different data per item; include primary key in each data object
validation.keys: must specify which items to update; update without keys will fail
validation.relational_update: data.relationField supports create (array), update (array with id), delete (array of ids) sub-keys

fields.keys: array of primary key UUIDs (required for same-data batch update)
fields.data: partial data object, or array of objects each with id for different-data batch update

provenance.quote:
[line 270] ### Before Updating Items

1. **Use keys parameter** for bulk updates with same data
2. **Use data array** for batch updates with different data per item
3. **Include primary key in data** when using batch update
4. **Check field permissions** - some fields may be read-only

---
[line 93] ### Updating Items

**✅ GOOD - Update with Keys:**

```json
{
	"action": "update",
	"collection": "posts",
	"keys": ["uuid-1", "uuid-2"],
	"data": { "status": "published" }
}
```

**✅ GOOD - Batch Update (Different Data):**

```json
{
	"action": "update",
	"collection": "posts",
	"data": [
		{ "id": "uuid-1", "title": "Updated Title 1" },
		{ "id": "uuid-2", "title": "Updated Title 2" }
	]
}
```

**✅ GOOD - Relational Updates:**

```json
{
	"action": "update",
	"collection": "posts",
	"keys": ["uuid-1"],
	"data": {
		"categories": {
			"create": [{ "name": "New Category" }],
			"update": [{ "id": 3, "name": "Renamed" }],
			"delete": [5]
		}
	}
… (+13 more lines)

fields.UUID: system identifier (e.g. abc-123-def-456); used in resolve, reject, flow, and operation key field in CRUD; auto-generated on creation; required for connecting operations
fields.key: human-readable name (e.g. send_email, check_status); defined by user on creation; used in data chain variables {{ operation_key }} to access results

provenance.quote:
## UUID vs Keys - Critical Distinction

**UUIDs** (System identifiers): `"abc-123-def-456"`

- Use in: `resolve`, `reject`, `flow`, operation `key` field in CRUD
- Generated automatically when operations are created
- Required for connecting operations

**Keys** (Human-readable names): `"send_email"`, `"check_status"`

- Use in: Data chain variables `{{ operation_key }}`
- You define these when creating operations
- Used to access operation results in subsequent operations </uuid_vs_keys>

<critical_syntax>

fields.data.name: display name (optional)
fields.data.resolve: UUID of next operation on success (required; null initially)
fields.data.reject: UUID of next operation on failure (required; null initially)

validation.condition_filters: use nested objects for filters (e.g. {"$trigger": {"payload": {"status": {"_eq": "published"}}}}) — never dot notation
validation.request_headers: array of objects [{"header": "Authorization", "value": "Bearer token"}] — not simple key-value objects
validation.request_body: stringified JSON string — not native objects
validation.data_chain: use operation key variables {{ operation_key }} — avoid $last (breaks when reordered)

provenance.quote:
## Critical Syntax Rules - MEMORIZE THESE

**Condition Filters**: Use nested objects, NEVER dot notation

- ❌ `"$trigger.payload.status"`
- ✅ `{"$trigger": {"payload": {"status": {"_eq": "published"}}}}`

**Request Headers**: Array of objects, NOT simple objects

- ❌ `{"Authorization": "Bearer token"}`
- ✅ `[{"header": "Authorization", "value": "Bearer token"}]`

**Request Body**: Stringified JSON, NOT native objects

- ❌ `"body": {"data": "value"}`
- ✅ `"body": "{\"data\": \"value\"}"`

**Data Chain Variables**: Use operation keys, avoid `$last`

- ❌ `{{ $last }}` (breaks when reordered)
- ✅ `{{ operation_key }}` (reliable) </critical_syntax>

<required_fields>

fields.condition: evaluates filter rules; options.filter uses nested object format with $trigger, payload etc.
fields.exec: custom JS/TS sandboxed (no file/network access); options.code is module.exports async function
fields.mail: options: to (array), subject, body; supports template type with template name and data

fields.item_relation.data.meta.one_allowed_coll: array of allowed target collection names
fields.item_relation.data.meta.junction_field: the other FK field in junction
fields.page_relation.data.meta.one_field: alias field name on the one-side

provenance.quote:
### M2A (Many-to-Any)

Items can relate to items from multiple different collections.

**Example**: Page blocks (hero, text, gallery)

**Complete M2A Workflow**:

1. **Create block collections** → Use `collections` tool to create each block type (e.g., `block_hero`, `block_text`,
   `block_gallery`) with UUID primary keys and specific fields

2. **Create junction collection** → Use `collections` tool to create `page_blocks` junction (hidden collection with UUID
   primary key)

3. **Add M2A field** → Use `fields` tool with `type: "alias"`, `special: ["m2a"]`, and `interface: "list-m2a"`

4. **Add junction fields** → Use `fields` tool to add `page` (UUID), `item` (string), `collection` (string), and `sort`
   (integer) fields to junction

5. **Create relations** (use `relations` tool):

```json
// Item relation (polymorphic)
{
  "action": "create",
  "collection": "page_blocks",
  "field": "item",
  "data": {
    "collection": "page_blocks",
    "field": "item",
    "related_collection": null,
    "meta": {
      "one_allowed_collections": ["block_hero", "block_text", "block_gallery"],
      "one_collection_field": "collection",
      "junction_field": "page_id"
    }
  }
}

// Page relation
… (+22 more lines)

fields.junction_collection: must be created first with UUID primary key
fields.first_relation.data.meta.one_field: alias field name on first collection
fields.first_relation.data.meta.junction_field: FK to second collection in junction
fields.second_relation.data.meta.one_field: alias field name on second collection
fields.second_relation.data.meta.junction_field: FK to first collection in junction

provenance.quote:
### M2O (Many-to-One)

Multiple items in one collection relate to one item in another.

**Example**: Many articles → one author

**Complete M2O Workflow**:

1. **Add M2O field** to the "many" collection → Use `fields` tool with `type: "uuid"` and
   `interface: "select-dropdown-m2o"` (see `fields` tool `<relationship_fields>` M2O example)

2. **Create relation** (use `relations` tool):

```json
{
	"action": "create",
	"collection": "articles",
	"field": "author",
	"data": {
		"collection": "articles",
		"field": "author",
		"related_collection": "directus_users",
		"schema": { "on_delete": "SET NULL" }
	}
}
```

</m2o_workflow>

<o2m_workflow>

fields.data.meta.one_field: alias field name on the one-side

provenance.quote:
### O2M (One-to-Many)

One item in a collection relates to many items in another collection.

**Example**: One author → many articles

**Complete O2M Workflow**:

1. **Add O2M field** to the "one" collection → Use `fields` tool with `type: "alias"`, `special: ["o2m"]`, and
   `interface: "list-o2m"`

2. **Create relation** connecting to existing M2O field (use `relations` tool):

```json
{
	"action": "create",
	"collection": "articles",
	"field": "author",
	"data": {
		"collection": "articles",
		"field": "author",
		"related_collection": "authors",
		"meta": {
			"one_field": "articles",
			"sort_field": null
		},
		"schema": {
			"on_delete": "SET NULL"
		}
	}
}
```

</o2m_workflow>

<m2m_workflow>

fields.multiple_files_M2M.junction_article_rela: alias field name on main collection

provenance.quote:
### File/Files Relationships

**Single File (M2O)**:

1. **Add file field** → Use `fields` tool with `type: "uuid"`, `special: ["file"]`, and `interface: "file"`

2. **Create relation** (use `relations` tool):

```json
{
	"action": "create",
	"collection": "articles",
	"field": "cover_image",
	"data": {
		"collection": "articles",
		"field": "cover_image",
		"related_collection": "directus_files",
		"schema": { "on_delete": "SET NULL" }
	}
}
```

**Multiple Files (M2M)**:

**Complete Files Workflow**:

1. **Create junction collection** → Use `collections` tool to create junction with UUID primary key

2. **Add files field** to main collection → Use `fields` tool with `type: "alias"`, `special: ["files"]`, and
   `interface: "files"` (see `fields` tool `<relationship_fields>` Files example)

3. **Add junction fields** → Use `fields` tool to add hidden `article_id` and `directus_files_id` (both UUID) fields to
   junction

4. **Create relations** (use `relations` tool):

```json
// Article relation
{
  "action": "create",
… (+35 more lines)

fields.one_field: field name in related collection (for O2M side)
fields.sort_field: enable manual sorting (typically an integer field)
fields.one_allowed_collections: array of collection names for M2A
fields.one_collection_field: field that stores collection name in M2A

provenance.quote:
### Meta Options

- `one_field`: Field name in related collection (for O2M side)
- `junction_field`: Opposite field in junction table
- `sort_field`: Enable manual sorting (typically an integer field)
- `one_deselect_action`: `nullify` or `delete`
- `one_allowed_collections`: Array of collection names for M2A
- `one_collection_field`: Field that stores collection name in M2A </relation_settings>

<common_patterns>

fields.junction_fields: singular form of related collection e.g. product_id, category_id
fields.alias_fields: plural form for many relations e.g. tags, categories

provenance.quote:
## Naming Conventions

- Junction collections: `{singular}_{plural}` (e.g., `product_categories`)
- Junction fields: Singular form of related collection (e.g., `product_id`, `category_id`)
- Alias fields: Plural form for many relations (e.g., `tags`, `categories`)
- M2O fields: Singular form (e.g., `author`, `brand`) </naming_conventions>

<related_tools>

fields.on_delete: CASCADE (default for M2M) | SET NULL (default for M2O) | NO ACTION | RESTRICT | SET DEFAULT

provenance.quote:
### Schema Options

- `on_delete`:
  - `CASCADE` (default for M2M) - Delete related items
  - `SET NULL` (default for M2O) - Set field to null
  - `NO ACTION` - Prevent deletion
  - `RESTRICT` - Prevent if related items exist
  - `SET DEFAULT` - Set to default value

- `on_update`:
  - Same options as `on_delete`

fields.keys: array of collection names to examine
fields.response: object keyed by collection name; each field has type, primary_key, readonly, required, interface (with type and choices), relation (with type: m2o|o2m|m2m|m2a and related_collections); JSON fields with repeaters/lists include nested fields structure recursively

fields.collections: alphabetically sorted array of real collection names (database tables)
fields.collection_folders: alphabetically sorted array of folder names (UI-only grouping, not real tables)
fields.notes: object of descriptions for collections and folders where available

validation.namespace: folders share same namespace as collections; check folders array before creating collections to avoid naming conflicts

provenance.quote:
### Discovery Mode (Default)

**Usage**: Call without parameters or with empty `keys` array

```json
{}
```

**Returns**: Lightweight schema overview

- `collections`: Alphabetically sorted array of real collection names (database tables)
- `collection_folders`: Alphabetically sorted array of folder names (UI-only, not real tables). Distinct from file
  folders. They are used for grouping different collections together in the UI.
- `notes`: Descriptions for both collections and folders (where available)

**Important**: Folders share the same namespace as collections. Before creating a new collection, check the `folders`
array to avoid naming conflicts (e.g., can't create a 'website' collection if a 'website' folder exists).

**Sample Response**:

```json
{
	"collections": ["categories", "contacts", "organizations", "pages", "posts", "products"],
	"collection_folders": ["content", "marketing", "website"],
	"notes": {
		"contacts": "People at the organizations you work with",
		"organizations": "Your clients and customers",
		"pages": "Static pages with page builder blocks",
		"posts": "Blog posts and articles",
		"content": "Content management folder"
	}
}
```

**Use case**: Initial exploration, getting oriented with available data structures

claims.subject: Trigger-flow / data access in triggered flow

provenance.quote:
## 📊 Data Access in Triggered Flow

The triggered flow receives:

- `$trigger.body` - The `data` parameter you send
- `$trigger.query` - The `query` parameter
- `$trigger.headers` - The `headers` parameter
- `$trigger.collection` - The collection context
- `$trigger.keys` - The selected item IDs
- `$accountability` - User/permission context

fields.keys: required when requireSelection is true or undefined; array of item IDs
fields.data: must include values for required fields defined in flow options.fields

fields.keys: optional (empty array allowed) when flow requireSelection is false

validation.flow_definition: must always read the flow definition before triggering
validation.flow_id_match: definition ID must match flowId parameter
validation.collection_support: collection must be in flow's collections array
validation.required_fields: all required fields must be present in data
validation.data: must be native objects not stringified JSON
validation.requireSelection: must be checked explicitly — do not assume its value

fields.collection_wildcard: '*' in collections means any collection is accepted
fields.for_each: if an array is passed, the flow runs once per array item
fields.response: flow can return data via its trigger's return option
fields.keys: array of item IDs (required if flow needs selection)

provenance.quote:
[line 108] ## 🔄 Validation Rules

The tool validates:

1. **Flow Definition**: Must provide complete flow object
2. **Flow ID Match**: Definition ID must match flowId parameter
3. **Collection Support**: Collection must be in flow's collections array
4. **Selection Required**: Keys required when `requireSelection !== false`
5. **Required Fields**: All required fields must be present in data

---
[line 185] ## ⚠️ Important Notes

- **Read First**: ALWAYS read the flow definition before triggering
- **Collection Wildcard**: `"*"` in collections means any collection is accepted
- **Required Fields**: Check `options.fields` for required inputs
- **For-Each**: If you pass an array, flow runs once per array item
- **Response**: Flow can return data via its trigger's `return` option
- **Permissions**: Respects flow's `accountability` setting

---
[line 13] ## Required Parameters

```json
{
	"flowDefinition": {}, // FULL flow object from flows.read
	"flowId": "uuid", // Flow ID to trigger
	"collection": "name", // Collection context
	"keys": ["id1"], // Item IDs (required if flow needs selection)
	"method": "POST", // GET or POST (default: GET)
	"data": {}, // Optional payload data
	"query": {}, // Optional query parameters
	"headers": {} // Optional headers
}
```

---
[line 194] ## 🚨 Common Mistakes to Avoid

1. **Don't** trigger without reading flow definition first
2. **Don't** omit keys when flow requires selection
3. **Don't** ignore required fields in flow configuration
4. **Don't** use wrong collection not in flow's collections list
5. **Don't** assume `requireSelection` - check explicitly
6. **Don't** pass stringified JSON - use native objects

additionalSources.quote:
## ⚠️ Important Notes

- **Read First**: ALWAYS read the flow definition before triggering
- **Collection Wildcard**: `"*"` in collections means any collection is accepted
- **Required Fields**: Check `options.fields` for required inputs
- **For-Each**: If you pass an array, flow runs once per array item
- **Response**: Flow can return data via its trigger's `return` option
- **Permissions**: Respects flow's `accountability` setting

## Required Parameters

```json
{
	"flowDefinition": {}, // FULL flow object from flows.read
	"flowId": "uuid", // Flow ID to trigger
	"collection": "name", // Collection context
	"keys": ["id1"], // Item IDs (required if flow needs selection)
	"method": "POST", // GET or POST (default: GET)
	"data": {}, // Optional payload data
	"query": {}, // Optional query parameters
	"headers": {} // Optional headers
}
```

## 🚨 Common Mistakes to Avoid

1. **Don't** trigger without reading flow definition first
2. **Don't** omit keys when flow requires selection
3. **Don't** ignore required fields in flow configuration
4. **Don't** use wrong collection not in flow's collections list
5. **Don't** assume `requireSelection` - check explicitly
6. **Don't** pass stringified JSON - use native objects

content.summary: Critical workflow order: (1) Create flow first using flows tool, (2) Create all operations with null resolve/reject initially using operations tool, (3) Link operations together using UUIDs from step 2, (4) Update flow to set first operation as entry point. Operations must exist before they can be referenced; UUIDs are only available after creation.

Flows can be chained: a child flow uses trigger type 'operation' with a return key, and a parent flow uses a trigger operation with the child flow UUID and payload. Child results are accessed via the trigger operation key. Best practice: name utilities with 'Utils ->' prefix, keep each utility focused on single responsibility.

A Flow consists of one trigger plus operations plus a data chain. Each flow has exactly ONE trigger that starts execution. Operations execute sequentially, passing data through the chain. The data chain accumulates results from each step.

provenance.quote:
## Key Concepts

**Flow** = Trigger + Operations + Data Chain

- Each flow has ONE trigger that starts execution
- Operations execute sequentially, passing data through the chain
- Data chain accumulates results from each step

After creating a flow, use the `operations` tool to add individual operations with detailed operation syntax,
positioning, and data chain usage. </flow_concepts>

<core_fields>

content.summary: The folders tool supports four actions: create (add new folder records), read (list/query metadata or get specific items by ID), update (modify existing metadata such as title, description, tags, folder), and delete (remove folders by keys).

provenance.quote:
## Available Actions

- `create`: Add new folders records
- `read`: List/query metadata or get specific items by ID
- `update`: Modify existing metadata (title, description, tags, folder)
- `delete`: Remove folders by keys

content.summary: The items tool supports four actions: read (query items with filtering/pagination/field selection), create (add items with nested relations), update (modify items with partial data), and delete (remove items by primary keys).

provenance.quote:
## Actions

- `read`: Query items with filtering/pagination/field selection
- `create`: Add items with nested relations
- `update`: Modify items with partial data
- `delete`: Remove items by primary keys

content.summary: Translations use a special M2M relationship with a languages collection. Workflow: (1) ensure languages collection exists, (2) create translations junction with UUID primary key, (3) add translations alias field with special: [translations] and interface: translations, (4) configure junction fields and relations following M2M pattern with languages collection.

provenance.quote:
### Translations

Special M2M relationship with `languages` collection.

**Complete Translations Workflow**:

1. **Ensure languages collection exists** (use `schema` tool to check)

2. **Create translations junction** → Use `collections` tool to create junction with UUID primary key

3. **Add translations field** → Use `fields` tool with `type: "alias"`, `special: ["translations"]`, and
   `interface: "translations"` (see `fields` tool `<relationship_fields>` Translations example)

4. **Configure junction fields and relations** → Follow M2M pattern with languages collection </translations_workflow>
   </relationship_types>

<relation_settings>

content.summary: The trigger-flow tool requires reading the flow first using the flows tool to get the full definition. Manual flows (trigger: manual) are designed to be triggered via UI or this tool. Any flow can be triggered, receiving data in $trigger.body. The tool validates collection support, required fields, and selection requirements.

provenance.quote:
## 🔑 Key Concepts

- **Prerequisite**: ALWAYS read the flow first using `flows` tool to get the full definition
- **Manual Flows**: Flows with `trigger: "manual"` are designed to be triggered via UI or this tool
- **Flow Chaining**: Any flow can be triggered, receiving data in `$trigger.body`
- **Validation**: Tool validates collection support, required fields, and selection requirements

content.summary: The fields tool is related to: collections (create containers for fields) and relations (connect fields between collections).

provenance.quote:
## Related Tools

- `collections`: Create containers for fields
- `relations`: Connect fields between collections </related_tools>

content.reason: the items tool cannot operate on directus_* system collections

provenance.quote:
## Restrictions

- Cannot operate on `directus_*` collections
- Respects user permissions/RBAC
- Delete operations may be environment-disabled
